constraint_categories.decision_needed: Which protected-area or conservation boundaries intersect the playable corridor and what detail may be published?
Which put-ins, take-outs, bridges, trails, and evacuation points are safe and appropriate to expose in-game?
Which areas should be generalized, hidden, or omitted from detailed maps and screenshots?
Which media can be used for texture, material, audio, animation, or review reference without violating rights?

editor_workflow.review_gate: Use approved first-party, official, or guide-reviewed geometry only; keep coordinates generalized for public previews until cleared.
Do not expose exact protected, cultural, habitat, access, or rescue-sensitive geometry in screenshots or packaged maps.
Review access and rescue feasibility separately for clear-season, rainfed-runnable, rainy-season-high, and flash-response conditions.
Capture reviewer, date, flow/rain context, publication limits, and allowed gameplay/art uses before promotion.

forbidden_use: shipping public route-detail screenshots without local and conservation review

metadata_layer_terms.promotion_gate: Do not import layer geometry, map tiles, time series, or derived masks until terms, CRS/axis order, attributes, corridor intersection, attribution, and guide/local review are recorded.

promotion_blockers: Exact put-in, take-out, bridge, road, trail, evacuation, and rescue geometries are not attached.
Protected-area, corridor, wetland, forest, recharge, and vulnerability layer intersections are metadata leads only.
Flow-dependent access and rescue behavior is not validated against rainfall/stage history or guide review.
Item-level rights for guide, outfitter, public, or social media references are not attached.
publication_gate: Record exact source layers and terms.
Attach guide or local review for access and safety-sensitive details.
Keep public/social media link-only until explicit rights are attached.
Do not package detailed conservation-sensitive coordinates until publication risk is reviewed.
source_rights_questions: Which protected areas or conservation rules intersect the Pacuare planning corridor?
Are any access points, cultural sites, or sensitive habitats unsuitable for detailed publication?
What attribution and redistribution terms apply to official GIS layers?
Which guide/outfitter media can be used for texture, material, animation, or audio reference?
